facts.notes: Confirmed by Return Transcript and filing package

facts.fact: The return claimed $250,000 in estimated tax payments on Line 26
IRS processed the return on March 31, 2025 (TC 150)
IRS placed TC 810 Refund Freeze on January 21, 2025
IRS Account Transcript does NOT show a $250,000 payment posting (TC 670)
facts.notes: No TC 670 for $250,000 visible on transcript

facts.fact: Certified mail confirms IRS received packages on Dec 5 2024, Jan 8 2025, Jan 28 2025

facts.fact: IRS issued Letter 3176C classifying the return as frivolous on June 3, 2026
IRS balance for 2024 is approximately $89,458 including penalties and interest

facts.fact: The $250,000 in vouchers were prepared as 1040-ES estimated payment vouchers
No EFTPS confirmation, cancelled check, or Treasury payment receipt has been located

facts.fact: Sovereign citizen theories (unilateral opt-out from government authority, A4V, lawful money redemption, birth certificate trust) have been consistently rejected by federal courts
facts.notes: ChatGPT analysis confirmed. Notice 2010-33 lists these as frivolous positions. Courts routinely dismiss these arguments.

facts.fact: Real constitutional and statutory protections exist for privacy, property rights, due process, and autonomy - these are separate from sovereign citizen theories
facts.notes: Due process (5th/14th Am), property rights, 4th Am search limits, Taxpayer Bill of Rights, trust law, business entities. These are real and usable.

facts.fact: The correct amount of 2024 federal tax cannot be determined until actual Schedule C income is reconstructed from bank statements and payment app records
facts.missing: Bank statements Jan-Dec 2024, PayPal/Cash App/Venmo/Stripe records, business expense documentation
facts.notes: The original return reported 50,799 net Schedule C income. This figure may be overstated or understated. Cannot determine correct tax until real income is verified.

facts.fact: Taxpayer is experiencing severe financial hardship - homeless, no steady income, no SSI, staying with friend Crystal Hall, relying on Medicaid
facts.missing: Form 433-F or 433-A to formally document hardship for IRS CNC status
facts.notes: This hardship directly supports the Currently Not Collectible (CNC) request under IRC 6343. Critical for stopping IRS collection while corrected return is prepared.
